content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 2A, 1990
t1s. Anne F. Patton
L447 Santa Fe Drive
Encinitas, cA 92024
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our FiLe No. I-9 0-68 3
Dear Ms. Patton:
This is in response to your request for informal advice
concerning your responsibilities under the conflict of interest
provisioni if tne potitica:- Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Because you
have requested informal advice and are not facing a specific
governm6ntal ' decision, we provide you with informal assistance.2
(Regulation 18329(c) r copY enclosed.) our advice is linited to
questions arising under the Political, Reform Act.
OUESTION
would you, as a menber of the design review board of the City
of Encinitas, face a conflict of interest by rneeting with city
staff and appearing before the city council- on behalf of a firm
that does business r,rith the city where your representation does
not involve issues which wilf come before the design review board?
CONCLUSTON
You nouLd not have a conf l-ict of interest unless, when
meeting with city staff or appearing before the city council, you
do so purportedly on behalf of the design review board.
1 
Go,r".rrnent Code Sections 81OOO-91015. 'A11 statutory
references are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 180oo, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.
2_
- 
fnformal- assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo . p.o. Box 80? . sacramento cA 958o4-o8oz . (916) 3 z2-s66o


Our File No.  I-90-683
Page 2
FACTS
You are an appointed menber of the city of Encinitas design
review board, which reviews development proposals and makes land
use decisions.
You are considering representinq, for compensation, a firm
that does business with the city. 
Your representation would not
involve l-and use decisions wj.thin the city. 
As a representative
of the firm, you hrould meet with city staff and appear before the
city council.
ANALYSTS
Section 87100 mandates that rrno public official 
at any level
of state or tocal government sha1I nake, participate in making or
in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmenlal decision in which he kno\"/s or has reason to know he
6as a financial interest. " 3
Thus, to ansr^rer your question, it is necessary to ans$'er
these guestions: (1) Are you a public official' under Section
871-oo?' (2) . what constitutes making, participating in or
attenptinq to j.nf fuence a goverrunental decision? (3) Do.you have
a fininciil 
interest based on the Proposed work for the firm?
I. 
Public Official
A public official" 
is every natural person who is a member
of a state or 1ocal, government agency. (Regulation 18700(a), coPY
enclosed. ) 
rrMemberrt incLudes unsalaried members of boards with
decision-making authority. 
(Regulation 18700 (a) (2) . ) 
rrl,ocal
government agencyrt includes every "subdivision, or any departnent,
division, bureau, office, board, commission" or other agency of
loca1 governments, including cities. (Section A2O4L. ) Under these
definiiions, you are a public official 
because you are a rnember of
a board that is part of a Local government agency with decision-
rnaking authority.
II. 
Governrnental Decision
A public official 
nakes a rrgovernnental decisionrt if, 
when
acting within the official 
authority of his or her position, the
official 
votes on a matter, appoints a person, obl j.gates or
commits his or her board or commission to a course of- action, or
3 tn addition to Section B71Oo, you should be aware of
84308. It discusses the lirnitations on appointed board
such as you, to participate in a decision if the rnernber
accepted, sol-icited or directed political contributions
party or participant in the case.
Sect ion
metnbers ,
has
from a


Our File No.  
■-90-683
Page 3
enters into a contract on its behalf. 
(Regulation 18700(b).) By
deciding not to do any of these things, the official 
would also be
naking a governmental decj,sion, unless the decision is based on a
aisquifiriing 
financial- interest and the interest is disclosed.4
A public official, I'participates in the makingT of a
governmLntal decisiontr if, when acting within the official
iuthority of his or her position, he or she negotiates with a
governmental entity, private person or bus j,ness entity regarding a
decision, or gives advice or recomnendations to the decision
maker, (Regulation L8700 (c) (2) . )
With reqard to a governmental decision that is within or
before the public official's 
own asencv, the official 
t'attempts.to
influence a governmental decisionrr if, for purposes of influencing
the decision, he or she contacts, appears before, or otherwj-se
attempts to influence any rnember, officer, employee or consultant
of his or her agency. (Regulation 18700.1(a), coPy encLosed.)
with regard to a governmental decision that is within or before a
governmental agency other than the public official's 
o$rn agency,
the official 
attempts to use his or her official 
position to
influence the decision if, "for the purpose of influencing the
decision, the official 
acts or purports to act on behalf of, or as
the representative of, his or her agency to any member, officer,
employLe or consuftant of an aqency. Such actions include, but
..L .rot lirnited to, the use of official 
stationery.ti 
(Regulation
187o0.L(c), copy enclosed. )
This last regulation is most relevant to your situation since
your question focuses on what conflicts might arise if you neet
wittr City staff and appear before the city council and not the
design review board. fn this context, you should be ar"rare that an
official 
is deerned to appear before his or her os/n agency not only
r.rhen actually appeari.ng before it but also when appearing before
an agency that was appointed by the official's 
own agency, and
when appearing before an aerency that has budgetary control over
the official's 
own agency (ReguLation 18700.1(a).) Here, the city
council- j,s not appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of
the design review board. consequently, an appearance before the
city council would be an appearance before an agency other than
your or.rn agency. Therefore, you vrould not be deemed to be
appearing before the design review board and thus you could appear
before the city council on behaLf of the firm on non-land use
matters which will not be corning before the design review board,
provided you do not purport to act on behal,f of the d6sign reviee,
board. (Regulation 18700.1(c); Levinqer Advice Letter, No. f-88-
328, wesi a6vice Letter, No. A-88-413, copies encIosed.15
* Specific disclosure requirenents are set forth in Regulation
18700 (b) (s) .
' 
Please keep in mind, however, that Regulation 18700.1(a),
quoted above, would apply if you were to meet with staff of the
design review board or appear before the desi.gn review board on
behalf of the f irm.


our File No.
Page 4
■-90-683
Your work for the firrn rnay, ho$rever, create a conflict of
interest for you if a decision on another matter subsequently
comes before the design review board and the decision has a
financial effect on the firm.
III. 
Economic fnterest
A public official 
has a financial interest in a governmental
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally, on the official 
or on a member of his or
her immediate family, or on a source of income to the official 
if
the source of incorne pays or promises to pay income totalling 
$250
or more $rithin the 12 months preceding the governmental decision.
(Section 87103 (c) . )
we do not know whether the firn will be a source of income to
you of $250 or more within L2 nonths preceding a decision by the
design review board. If it is, you would be required to
disqualify yourself, provided it is reasonably foreseeable that
the board decision in question wilI naterially affect the f irrn.
Iv. Foreseeabilitv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood they will, occur. To be foreseeable,
the effects of a decision must be more than a nere possibility,
though certainty is not required. 
(Do!'rnev cares v. Downey
Development Com. (1987) 196 CaI.App.3d 817, 983, 989-991; witt v'
Morro$, (L977) 70 Cal-.App.3d 8L7,822; In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC
Ops. 198. )
v. 
Materialitv
whether a decision naterially affects . .o.r.l" of incorne
turns on whether the econornic interest is directlv or indirectly
involved in the decision. A source of income is directly involved
in a decision if it initiates the proceeding in which the decision
will be made by filing a clairn, application, appeal or sirnilar
request, or is a narned party in or subject of the proceeding.5
The decision is deemed to have a naterial effect on a financial-
interest if the interest is directly involved. For exarnple, j.f
the firrn appears before the design review board, it lrould be
directly involved and you could not participate in a ilecision on
that matter.
A person or business entity is the subject of a proceeding
a decision involves the issuance, renesral, ipproval, denial or
revocation.of any license, permit, or other entitlernent to, or
contract with the subject business entity. 
Regulation
L8'702.1(d).)
if


In addition, a governmental decj-sion is material j,f a rrnexusrt
exists between it and the purpose for which the official 
receives
income. A nexus exists if the official 
receives income to achieve
a goal or purpose that would be achieved, defeated, aided or
hindered by the decision. (Regulation la7o2.1(d).) 
we do not
have enough inforrnation about the firm or decisions before the
board to say whether a nexus exists.
rf the decision involves the firn only indirectly, you sti1l
night face a conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.2 (copy
enclosed). It expLains the occasions $rhen a decision will have a
rnaterial financial effect on a business entity in which a pubtic
official 
has an econornic interest. 
The analysis under Regulation
La7o2.2 requires an exa:nination of the financial situation of the
business entity and the effect the decision will have on the
entity. 
we do not have enough information to conduct this
analysis. Thus, we cannot say whether you r^rouId face a conflict
under Regulation 18702.2.
I hope this fetter has answered your question. If you have
any other questions please, do not hesitate to call me at (915)
322-590r.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel

I-90-683
B
Counsel, Legal Division
